mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 12% signals tightening labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 12%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production and housing starts indicates expanding real economic activity that drives corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no significant correlation with combined INDPRO/HOUST growth (Pearson r < 0.15) in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in GDP growth below 2% signals weakening demand conditions that increase equity risk premiums, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where GDPC1 growth falls below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in CPI inflation above 3% triggers central bank hawkishness that raises real interest rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL rises above 3%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in payroll employment below 100k indicates weak labor market performance that reduces consumer spending confidence, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS growth falls below 100k.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in consumer spending (RSAFS) and GDP growth (GDPC1) signals deteriorating demand conditions that increase equity volatility, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no significant correlation with combined RSAFS/GDPC1 decline (Pearson r > -0.15) in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly contraction in industrial production below 90% of its 12-month moving average indicates weakening supply chain capacity that reduces corporate margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO falls below 90% of its 12mo MA.
